meta.milestone_note: GDP arrays are indexed to these years. Engine must interpolate linearly between milestone indices for yearly steps.

gdp_world_trillions.note: Sum of 10 modeled countries plus estimated rest-of-world. Nominal USD — not inflation-adjusted.

global_gini_between_countries.note: Inter-country Gini weighted by population — not within-country inequality

gdp_trillions.note: 2026 value corrected to $28.5T per seed_data.json. index.html had $32.4T — see flag F001.
2026 value corrected to $19.2T per seed_data.json. index.html had $20.9T — see flag F002.
2026 value of $0.4T is close to 1990 value of $0.3T — stagnation period 1990-2026 plausible given instability. 2100 target is very aggressive — see flag F007.

world_population_billions.source: UN World Population Prospects — medium variant

oil.note: Does not account for new discoveries or unconventional sources

global_co2_concentration_ppm.note: Post-2075 decline assumes large-scale carbon capture deployment — see flag F012

resource_drag_threshold.note: When fossil_fuel_remaining falls below this value, a resource_drag penalty should be applied to GDP growth proportional to energy intensity of each country.

global_democracy_index.note: Dip at 2026 reflects documented autocratization trend. Recovery assumes climate cooperation pressure.

multilateral_cooperation_index.note: Low in 2026 reflects fragmentation of international institutions

tfp_multiplier.note: Total Factor Productivity — primary long-run GDP growth multiplier. Must be wired into engine growth rate calculation.

ai_capability_index.note: Approaches ceiling after assumed AGI transition circa 2040–2050. Diminishing marginal returns modeled.

renewable_tech_cost_index.note: Learning curve model. Values below 1.0 mean cheaper than 1990 baseline.

biotech_health_multiplier.note: Captures genomic medicine, mRNA platforms, longevity research

automation_labor_displacement_pct.note: Displacement does not equal unemployment — assumes labor market transformation over 20–30 year lag

assumptions: All GDP values are nominal USD trillions. No inflation deflator applied. Inter-period comparisons spanning decades will be distorted by cumulative inflation.
Milestone interpolation between the six keyframe years (1990, 2026, 2038, 2075, 2080, 2100) should use piecewise-linear or smooth-spline interpolation for yearly steps in the engine.
Technology TFP multiplier acts as a multiplicative modifier on the base GDP growth rate and must be explicitly wired into engine.js.
Resource constraints trigger a growth drag coefficient when fossil_fuel_remaining_pct falls below 30% or water_stress_index exceeds 0.6.
Demographic aging increases healthcare costs and reduces labor force productivity. engine.js Rule B encodes this but must be extended to all countries.
Political stability (multilateral_cooperation_index) positively modulates technology adoption speed and trade openness.
India GDP of $95T by 2100 requires ~4.1% average annual real growth sustained over 74 years — plausible with institutional stability and demographic dividend but sensitive to shocks.
Nigeria GDP growth from $0.4T (2026) to $35T (2100) implies ~14.6% nominal annual growth. This is speculative and requires a modeled governance improvement trajectory.
China population declines post-2026 due to sub-replacement TFR of 1.2. Labor force shrinkage must drag GDP growth in engine.
Japan GDP grows modestly from $4.4T to $8.0T despite severe aging and population decline — assumes high productivity gains from automation offsetting demographic drag.
Climate scenario is moderate mitigation (approx. RCP4.5), not business-as-usual. CO2 decline post-2075 assumes large-scale carbon capture deployment.
Automation displacement is modeled with a 20–30 year adaptation lag before new job categories absorb displaced workers. No structural mass unemployment assumed in baseline.
Rest-of-world GDP is included in global totals as an estimated aggregate and is not explicitly broken out by country.

flags.description: USA 2026 GDP: index.html has $32.4T but seed_data.json has $28.5T. Real 2024 US GDP is ~$28T nominal, making $28.5T for 2026 the correct figure. $32.4T looks like a ~2030 projection or a PPP-adjusted value.
flags.recommendation: Correct index.html BASELINE_DATA[0].g[1] from 32.4 to 28.5. Adopted $28.5T in this baseline.

flags.description: China 2026 GDP: index.html has $20.9T but seed_data.json has $19.2T. China nominal GDP 2024 is ~$18T, so $19.2T for 2026 is plausible. $20.9T is slightly high.
flags.recommendation: Correct index.html BASELINE_DATA[1].g[1] from 20.9 to 19.2. Adopted $19.2T in this baseline.

flags.description: Loop condition `year < 2100` excludes year 2100 from simulation. A 74-year simulation from 2026 should run through and including 2100.
flags.recommendation: Change `year < 2100` to `year <= 2100` on line 16 of engine.js.

flags.description: Demographic aging (retiredShare increment of +0.2/year) is hardcoded only for USA and CHN. India is excluded despite having a significant aging trajectory. The 7 countries in index.html not in seed_data.json receive no demographic modeling at all.
flags.recommendation: Move aging rate to a per-country parameter in seed_data.json (e.g., `agingRatePerYear`). Apply generically in engine. Add all 10 countries to seed_data.json.

flags.description: engine.js models only two growth modifiers (debt penalty, aging drag). Technology TFP, resource depletion, political stability, and climate impacts have no causal link to GDP in the engine — making the society/resources/politics/technology domains decorative.
flags.recommendation: Add to each year's growth calculation: +tfp_boost (from technology.tfp_multiplier derivative), -resource_drag (when fossil reserves < threshold), +/-governance_modifier (from politics.multilateral_cooperation_index).

flags.description: seed_data.json only contains 3 countries (USA, CHN, IND). index.html has 10 countries. The 7 missing countries (IDN, NGA, PAK, BGD, BRA, MEX, JPN) cannot be simulated by engine.js.
flags.recommendation: Add all 10 countries to seed_data.json using the baseline parameters in this file's world.economy.countries array.

flags.description: Nigeria GDP growth from $0.4T (2026) to $35T (2100) implies ~14.6% nominal annual growth — far exceeding any sustained historical precedent. No governance improvement mechanism exists in the model to justify this trajectory.
flags.recommendation: Add a governance multiplier gating emerging-market growth. Revise Nigeria 2100 target to $10–15T as a moderate scenario; $35T as an optimistic upper bound only.

flags.description: All GDP values are nominal. Over 110 years (1990–2100), cumulative inflation will distort all cross-period comparisons by a large factor. There is no deflator or constant-price series.
flags.recommendation: Add a `global_price_level_index` series to world.economy, or explicitly document that all GDP values are in current-year nominal USD and cannot be directly compared across time without deflating.

flags.description: Resource constraints (fossil depletion, water stress, land utilization) have no feedback loop into the GDP growth calculation. Without this, GDP projections are unconstrained by physical limits.
flags.recommendation: Implement a resource_drag coefficient in engine.js: when fossil_fuel_remaining < 30%, subtract a drag proportional to each country's energy intensity from the growth rate.

flags.description: workingAgeShare + retiredShare does not sum to 100% for any country (USA: 80%, CHN: 86.5%, IND: 77.2%). The implied third share — youth/child population — is unnamed and missing from seed_data.json.
flags.recommendation: Add `youthShare` field to each country in seed_data.json. Verify all three shares sum to 100. Values estimated in this baseline's youth_share_pct fields.

flags.description: No stochastic shock mechanism exists. A deterministic 74-year projection will underestimate variance and give false precision. Recessions, pandemics, wars, and breakthrough technologies are unmodeled.
flags.recommendation: Add an optional `shocks` module with parameterized probability distributions and magnitude ranges for major event types (recession, pandemic, war, tech leap, climate event).

flags.description: CO2 concentration declining from 470ppm (2075) to 450ppm (2100) requires carbon capture at a scale not demonstrated at any point in history. This is an optimistic assumption embedded in the baseline.
flags.recommendation: Gate CO2 decline on a boolean scenario flag `carbon_capture_deployed`. In the neutral baseline, hold CO2 flat at ~470ppm post-2075 rather than declining.
